title: Require fail-closed per-unit custom-op final-gate evidence
trigger_fingerprint: CUDA-to-NPU custom-op migration reaches final validation with summary-only or incomplete final-gate evidence: missing/non-true project_e2e_passed or report_parity_passed, absent rows, missing migration_manifest.required_units, zero/absent runtime coverage, missing parity/performance/no-fallback proof, or any required custom-op unit without CLOSED_PASS evidence.

title: Port CUDA custom extensions to NPU-routed C++/ATen extensions without changing the Python API
trigger_fingerprint: CUDA custom extension or pybind operator fails Phase 5/operator validation due to missing NPU-native implementation/build/runtime evidence; existing Python API must remain unchanged; CUDAExtension/nvcc or CUDA kernel wrappers are present; CPU fallback/deleting extension is disallowed; operator semantics can be expressed with torch-npu-supported C++/ATen tensor operations on PrivateUse1/NPU tensors.

source_runs: e2e-v2-f3828806dcb5-exp-Hoist torch_npu imports and enforce NPU-only device selection
title: Hoist torch_npu imports and enforce NPU-only device selection

anti_patterns: placing backend imports inside except blocks or interactive loops
preserving CUDA device selection after NPU migration
positive_signals: import torch with injected import torch_npu
torch_npu import appears in nested or exception-handler scope
IndentationError or syntax failure after import injection
device-selection logic contains CPU or CUDA fallback
validation entrypoint must run only on Ascend NPU
required_rule_actions: hoist import torch_npu to module-level imports near import torch
insert immediate torch.npu availability gate after imports
raise RuntimeError when NPU is unavailable
set active runtime device to npu:0
rerun syntax/import validation and migrated runtime command

title: Fail closed on Ascend NPU availability in validation entrypoints

applicability_conditions: entry command is the binding validation surface
success requires actual NPU execution rather than generic PyTorch availability
processor inputs and generated tensors must follow model.device
core_actions: import torch_npu inside a device detection helper
raise on missing torch_npu or torch_npu.npu.is_available() false
return only the NPU device path
validate with final exit_code 0 and explicit NPU-use evidence
problem_signals: validation or inference entrypoint contains cuda/cpu fallback
migration constraints require Ascend NPU-only execution
torch_npu stack is expected but may be missing or unavailable
large model validation could falsely pass on CPU/CUDA or fail with OOM
